title: Delicate Aerated Syrian Potato Pilaf
description: A rich and energizing Syrian dish featuring charlotte potatoes, this pilaf is both ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly and delicious, with a light and airy texture from the use of brine-wet potatoes.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the diced potatoes, salt, black pepper, cumin, and coriander to the saucepan.
Pour in the warm brine and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Fluff the pilaf with a fork to separate the potatoes and create a light and airy texture.
Serve the pilaf hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
